name: docs_batch_update
description: Apply a raw Google Docs API batchUpdate to a document for full structural editing (insert/format/replace text, tables, images, paragraph styles, etc). You supply the requests array exactly as the Google Docs API expects it; it is passed through verbatim. Use read_doc first to get the element index map so you can target ranges precisely.

document_id.description: The Google Docs document ID to edit.

requests.description: a Google Docs API batchUpdate `requests` array (full structural editing). Each item is one Docs request object (e.g. insertText, updateTextStyle, insertTable, replaceAllText).

operator.description: Optional. If omitted, resolves to the BlackBox's current operator (single operator auto-selected; otherwise the system default). Do not hard-code an operator name.

returns: JSON with document_id, applied (request count), and the API replies array (or an error string).
notes:
requests is the RAW Google Docs API array, passed through verbatim. Worked examples:

1) Insert text at the top of the doc:
[{"insertText": {"location": {"index": 1}, "text": "Project Plan\n"}}]

2) Bold a range (get startIndex/endIndex from read_doc):
[{"updateTextStyle": {"range": {"startIndex": 1, "endIndex": 13}, "textStyle": {"bold": true}, "fields": "bold"}}]

3) Insert a 2x3 table at index 1:
[{"insertTable": {"location": {"index": 1}, "rows": 2, "columns": 3}}]

Indices shift after inserts within the same batch — order requests carefully (often insert later positions first, or insert content before styling it).
